title: Notes App
description: Ask the user for 3 notes. Write them to 'notes.txt' (one per line). Then read the file and display each note with a line number.
starterCode:
# Get 3 notes
n1 = input("Note 1: ")
n2 = input("Note 2: ")
n3 = input("Note 3: ")

# Write to file

# Read and display
solution:
n1 = input("Note 1: ")
n2 = input("Note 2: ")
n3 = input("Note 3: ")

with open("notes.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write(n1 + "\n")
    f.write(n2 + "\n")
    f.write(n3 + "\n")

with open("notes.txt", "r") as f:
    for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
        print(f"{i}. {line}", end="")

testCases.label: Adding notes
testCases.input:
Learn Python
Practice daily
Build projects
testCases.expectedOutput:
Note 1: Note 2: Note 3: 1. Learn Python
2. Practice daily
3. Build projects

title: File Copy
description: Read all lines from 'source.txt' (create it first) and write them to 'copy.txt' with line numbers. Print the contents of copy.txt.
starterCode:
# Create source file
with open("source.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Line A\nLine B\nLine C\n")

# Read and copy with numbers
solution:
with open("source.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Line A\nLine B\nLine C\n")

with open("source.txt", "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

with open("copy.txt", "w") as f:
    for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
        f.write(f"{i}: {line}")

with open("copy.txt", "r") as f:
    print(f.read())

testCases.label: Copied with numbers
testCases.expectedOutput:
1: Line A
2: Line B
3: Line C
